_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Star Wars Jedi Academy
author: akiondev
description:
Star Wars Jedi Academy dedicated server (linuxjampded) running on a 32-bit Debian (i386) Docker image.

Features:
- Runs the original 32-bit linuxjampded binary inside a Debian i386 container.
- Automatically downloads the dedicated server binaries from the jknexus-basejka repository.
- Accepts a single URL pointing to an archive containing the base game assets (*.pk3 files).
- Supports ZIP, tar.gz, tar.bz2, tar.xz, plain tar, 7z and RAR archives — extracted automatically into the base/ folder.

Requirements:
- You must own a legal copy of Star Wars Jedi Academy.
- Provide the URL to an archive of your base assets (assets0.pk3, assets1.pk3, assets2.pk3) in the ASSETS_URL variable.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Jedi Academy Dedicated Server — Pterodactyl install script
#
# What this script does:
#  1. Downloads the dedicated server binaries from the jknexus-basejka repo.
#  2. Downloads the archive pointed to by ASSETS_URL.
#  3. Detects the archive type and extracts it into /mnt/server/base/.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

REPO_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/akiondev/jknexus-basejka/main/jalinuxded_1.011"

echo "-------------------------------------------"
echo " JKA Dedicated Server — Install Script"
echo "-------------------------------------------"

# Install extra tools needed for archive extraction
echo "Installing archive utilities..."
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq file p7zip-full unrar-free curl wget

# Create directory structure
echo "Creating server directory structure..."
mkdir -p /mnt/server/base

# Download dedicated server binaries
echo "Downloading linuxjampded..."
curl -fsSL -o /mnt/server/linuxjampded "${REPO_RAW}/linuxjampded"

echo "Downloading jampgamei386.so..."
curl -fsSL -o /mnt/server/base/jampgamei386.so "${REPO_RAW}/jampgamei386.so"


echo "Downloading example server.cfg..."
curl -fsSL -o /mnt/server/base/server.cfg "${REPO_RAW}/server.cfg"

chmod +x /mnt/server/linuxjampded

# Download base assets archive
if [ -z "${ASSETS_URL:-}" ]; then
    echo "WARNING: ASSETS_URL is not set. Skipping base assets download."
    echo "You must manually place assets0.pk3, assets1.pk3 and assets2.pk3 in the base/ folder."
else
    echo "Downloading base assets from: ${ASSETS_URL}"
    ASSETS_TMP="/tmp/jka_assets_download"
    curl -fsSL -o "${ASSETS_TMP}" "${ASSETS_URL}"

    # Detect the archive type using 'file' and the URL extension
    MIME_TYPE=$(file --mime-type -b "${ASSETS_TMP}")
    FILE_LOWER=$(echo "${ASSETS_URL}" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed 's/[?#].*//')

    echo "Detected MIME type: ${MIME_TYPE}"

    EXTRACT_DIR="/tmp/jka_assets_extract"
    mkdir -p "${EXTRACT_DIR}"

    if echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.zip$' || [ "${MIME_TYPE}" = "application/zip" ]; then
        echo "Extracting ZIP archive..."
        unzip -q -o "${ASSETS_TMP}" -d "${EXTRACT_DIR}"
    elif echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.(tar\.gz|tgz)$' || [ "${MIME_TYPE}" = "application/gzip" ]; then
        echo "Extracting tar.gz archive..."
        tar -xzf "${ASSETS_TMP}" -C "${EXTRACT_DIR}"
    elif echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.(tar\.bz2|tbz2)$' || [ "${MIME_TYPE}" = "application/x-bzip2" ]; then
        echo "Extracting tar.bz2 archive..."
        tar -xjf "${ASSETS_TMP}" -C "${EXTRACT_DIR}"
    elif echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.(tar\.xz|txz)$' || [ "${MIME_TYPE}" = "application/x-xz" ]; then
        echo "Extracting tar.xz archive..."
        tar -xJf "${ASSETS_TMP}" -C "${EXTRACT_DIR}"
    elif echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.tar$' || [ "${MIME_TYPE}" = "application/x-tar" ]; then
        echo "Extracting tar archive..."
        tar -xf "${ASSETS_TMP}" -C "${EXTRACT_DIR}"
    elif echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.7z$' || [ "${MIME_TYPE}" = "application/x-7z-compressed" ]; then
        echo "Extracting 7z archive..."
        7z x "${ASSETS_TMP}" -o"${EXTRACT_DIR}" -y
    elif echo "${FILE_LOWER}" | grep -qE '\.(rar)$' || echo "${MIME_TYPE}" | grep -q 'rar'; then
        echo "Extracting RAR archive..."
        unrar x -o+ "${ASSETS_TMP}" "${EXTRACT_DIR}/"
    else
        echo "Unknown or ambiguous archive type (${MIME_TYPE}), trying generic extraction..."
        7z x "${ASSETS_TMP}" -o"${EXTRACT_DIR}" -y \
            || tar -xf "${ASSETS_TMP}" -C "${EXTRACT_DIR}" \
            || unzip -q -o "${ASSETS_TMP}" -d "${EXTRACT_DIR}"
    fi

    # Move all .pk3 files found anywhere in the extracted tree into base/
    echo "Moving .pk3 files to /mnt/server/base/..."
    find "${EXTRACT_DIR}" -type f -iname '*.pk3' -exec mv -v {} /mnt/server/base/ \;

    # Clean up temp files
    rm -rf "${ASSETS_TMP}" "${EXTRACT_DIR}"

    echo "Base assets installed."
fi

echo ""
echo "-------------------------------------------"
echo " Installation complete!"
echo "-------------------------------------------"
echo ""
echo "Server files:"
ls -la /mnt/server/
echo ""
echo "Base assets:"
ls -la /mnt/server/base/

variables.name: Base Assets URL
variables.description: URL to a ZIP or archive file containing the base game assets (assets0.pk3, assets1.pk3, assets2.pk3). Supported formats: .zip, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tar.bz2, .tar.xz, .tar, .7z, .rar. The .pk3 files are automatically extracted and placed in the base/ folder.
